title: 11 Herbal Decoctions to Tame an Upset Stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for upset stomach because it helps to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the gut, relieving symptoms such as nausea and bloating.
Shogaol is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines.
Zingiberene is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce irritation, leading to relief from stomach discomfort and nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale decoctions, also known as ginger, can be good for an upset stomach. When you eat something bad or get a stomach bug, it can cause nausea, vomiting, and pain. Ginger's active compounds, like gingerol and shogaol, help to relax the stomach muscles and reduce inflammation. This makes it easier for your body to digest food and get rid of the bad stuff. Ginger also has natural antispasmodic properties, which help to calm down stomach contractions and prevent further discomfort. Additionally, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce irritation. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the stomach muscles, ginger decoctions can provide quick relief from stomach upset and help to restore balance to your digestive system.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Valeriana officinalis herbal decoctions that help with an upset stomach, start by gathering the roots of the valerian plant, which is also known as valerian root. You can purchase these roots from a herbal store or grow them yourself in a garden. Next, clean and chop the roots into small pieces, making sure to remove any dirt or debris. Then, fill a saucepan with water and add the chopped roots, making sure they are completely covered. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for about 10-15 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced and the roots are soft. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the roots. You can store the decoction in the fridge for up to 3 days or freeze it for later use. To take the decoction, mix 1-2 tablespoons with water and drink it as needed to help soothe an upset stomach.

Triterpenoids help an upset stomach by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, which can ease cramps, bloating, and discomfort.

Saponins, also known as valerian root, are good for an upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the digestive system and reduce irritation and discomfort in the stomach and intestines.
Licorinic acid is good for an upset stomach because it helps to soothe the digestive system and calm inflammation in the gut, making it easier to digest food and reducing nausea and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions, made from the roots of the licorice plant, can help soothe an upset stomach. This is because the decoction contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Glycyrrhizin helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can cause pain and discomfort. It also helps to reduce stomach acid, which can irritate the stomach and cause heartburn and acid reflux. When taken as a decoction, the active compounds in Glycyrrhiza glabra can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has been traditionally used to help with digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, by soothing the digestive tract and promoting healthy gut bacteria.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Valeriana officinalis herbal decoction for an upset stomach, start by gathering dried valerian root, which can be found at most health food stores or online. Next, measure out one teaspoon of dried root for every cup of water you plan to use, and add the root to a small saucepan. Pour in water, making sure the root is completely covered, and bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 5-10 minutes, allowing the active compounds from the valerian root to infuse into the water. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for another 10-15 minutes, allowing the mixture to cool down and the flavors to meld together. Finally, strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids, and drink the liquid as needed to help soothe an upset stomach and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with digestive problems. The seeds of the plant contain a natural compound called anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach. When you drink a decoction made from fennel seeds, the anethole is released into your body, where it can help calm down the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. This can help ease symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating, gas, and stomach cramps. The fennel decoction can also help stimulate digestion and improve the movement of food through the digestive system, which can help prevent constipation and other digestive problems.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Valeriana officinalis herbal decoction that helps with an upset stomach, start by gathering the right materials. You will need dried Valerian root, a saucepan, a cup, and water. Begin by measuring out one teaspoon of dried Valerian root for every cup of water you plan to use. Add the root to the saucepan and pour in the water, making sure the root is completely covered. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 5-10 minutes. The longer it cooks, the stronger the decoction will be. Once the decoction has cooled, strain it into a cup to remove the root. You can drink the decoction as is, or add a small amount of honey if you prefer it sweeter. It's best to drink the decoction before bedtime to help with digestion and promote a good night's sleep.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a popular herb used for treating an upset stomach. When made into a decoction, its active compounds are easily absorbed by the body. The main compound, menthol, helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation. This can help to alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and bloating that often come with an upset stomach. By calming the digestive system, peppermint decoction can also help to speed up digestion and reduce cramps. Additionally, its calming effect on the stomach can also help to reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) like abdominal pain and diarrhea. People often find that drinking peppermint decoction helps to soothe their stomach and provide relief from discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Valeriana officinalis herbal decoction that helps with an upset stomach, start by gathering dried root of the valerian plant, which can be bought from a health food store. Next, boil one teaspoon of the dried root in two cups of water for about 10-15 minutes, then reduce the heat and let it simmer. After 15 minutes, turn off the heat and let the decoction steep for another 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid using a fine mesh or cheesecloth into a clean container, then discard the solids. Once the decoction has cooled down, you can drink it as needed to help soothe an upset stomach and promote relaxation. It is recommended to drink one to two cups of the decoction per day, but it's best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis decoction is a natural remedy that can help soothe an upset stomach. The decoction may also help regulate bowel movements and improve digestion, making it easier to eat and digest food. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Valeriana officinalis herbal decoction that helps with an upset stomach, start by gathering dried valerian root, which can be found in most health food stores or online. Begin by measuring one to two teaspoons of dried valerian root for every cup of water you will be using. Next, combine the valerian root with water in a saucepan and place it on low heat. As the mixture simmers, you will notice a fragrant and slightly sweet aroma filling the air. You can now drink the decoction as needed to help soothe an upset stomach and promote relaxation. remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis decoctions are made from the roots of the valerian plant. This plant has been used for centuries to help people with upset stomachs. The reason is that valerian root has a calming effect on the digestive system. It helps to slow down digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach. This can be helpful when you have an upset stomach from eating something that doesn't agree with you. The valerian root also helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can become tight and painful when you have a stomach upset. Drinking a decoction of valerian root can help to soothe and calm the stomach, making the symptoms of an upset stomach less severe. The valerian root is also a natural antispasmodic, which means it can help to prevent the stomach from churning and making you feel worse.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Valeriana officinalis herbal decoction that helps with an upset stomach, start by gathering dried roots of the valerian plant, which can be found in most herbal stores or online. Next, measure out one teaspoon of the dried roots for every cup of water you plan to use. Then, boil fresh water in a saucepan and add the measured valerian roots. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the roots to release their medicinal properties into the water. After the steeping time has passed, strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids. Let the decoction cool slightly before consuming it. It is recommended to drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the decoction 2-3 times a day to help soothe an upset stomach and promote relaxation. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have underlying health conditions or take medications regularly.

intro_desc: An upset stomach can be a real nuisance, making it hard to enjoy your favorite foods and activities. But, there's good news - herbal decoctions can help soothe and calm your stomach. One of the best herbs for this is ginger, or 'Zingiber officinale'. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and ease digestive discomfort. When you drink ginger tea, the warm liquid can help relax your stomach muscles and improve digestion. Another herb that's great for upset stomachs is licorice root, or 'Glycyrrhiza glabra'. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm irritation in the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and discomfort.  It's essential to address an upset stomach because it can disrupt your daily life. If you're constantly feeling queasy or uncomfortable after eating, you might start to avoid your favorite foods, which can lead to nutrient deficiencies and a limited diet. This can make it harder to focus at work or school, and even impact your mood and energy levels. By using herbal decoctions to soothe your stomach, you can start to feel better and get back to your normal routine. This can improve your life in many ways, such as allowing you to eat a balanced diet, participate in activities you enjoy, and feel more energized and focused. By incorporating herbal decoctions into your daily routine, you can start to feel better and take control of your digestive health.
supplementary_best: To remedy an upset stomach, a combination of Zingiber officinale (ginger) and Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root) decoctions is recommended. Ginger helps to ease nausea and reduce inflammation in the stomach. Licorice root soothes the stomach lining, reducing irritation and inflammation. Foeniculum vulgare (fennel) is also added to this combination as it aids in digestion and reduces bloating. Fennel's carminative properties help to eliminate gas from the digestive system.  The combination of these three decoctions is beneficial because ginger and licorice root work together to reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining, while fennel helps to eliminate gas and aid in digestion. This balance of actions helps to calm the stomach and promote a sense of comfort.
supplementary_avoid: If you have an upset stomach, there are some herbal decoctions you should avoid.  One of these is decoctions made from 'Sassafras albidum', as they can cause stomach cramps and diarrhea. The main concern is that Sassafras contains a compound called safrole, which can be toxic to the liver and exacerbate stomach issues.  Another is decoctions made from 'Hydrastis canadensis', as they can irritate the stomach lining and worsen stomach problems. This herb contains berberine, which can be harsh on the digestive system.  You should also avoid decoctions made from 'Taxus brevifolia', as they contain taxine, a toxic compound that can cause severe stomach pain, nausea, and vomiting.  Additionally, decoctions made from 'Rauvolfia serpentina' should be avoided, as they can cause stomach upset and interact with certain medications. This herb can also lower blood pressure and worsen conditions like low blood pressure.  Finally, decoctions made from 'Datura stramonium' should be avoided, as they can cause stomach cramps, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal problems due to the presence of scopolamine.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger is another natural remedy that can help with an upset stomach. When you eat ginger, it helps to calm down the muscles in your stomach that can cause cramps and nausea. This is because ginger has a special compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the stomach. Drinking ginger tea or eating ginger in small amounts can help to soothe your stomach and make you feel better. Probiotics are also helpful in this situation. Probiotics are good bacteria that live in your gut and help to break down the food you eat. When you take probiotics, they can help to restore the balance of good bacteria in your gut, which can help to alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach. Apple cider vinegar is another natural remedy that can help with an upset stomach. When you drink apple cider vinegar, it can help to stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach. This is because apple cider vinegar has a special compound called acetic acid, which can help to break down food and reduce symptoms of an upset stomach.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have an upset stomach often experience other ailments like diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting. These symptoms can be caused by a weakened digestive system, which can be due to various factors such as food poisoning, stress, or an imbalance of gut bacteria. Medicinal herbs like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile can help alleviate these symptoms. Peppermint, for example, has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce nausea. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help calm the stomach and reduce vomiting. Chamomile, a calming herb, can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can help ease digestive issues.  Other common ailments experienced by people with an upset stomach include irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), bloating, and stomach cramps. These symptoms can be caused by a range of factors, including food intolerances, hormonal changes, and anxiety. Medicinal herbs like licorice root, dandelion root, and meadowsweet can help alleviate these symptoms. Licorice root, for instance, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce stomach cramps. Dandelion root can help stimulate digestion and reduce bloating. Meadowsweet, a natural antacid, can help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation.  In some cases, people with an upset stomach may also experience fatigue, headaches, and a general feeling of unwellness. These symptoms can be caused by a lack of essential nutrients and electrolytes, which can be due to a weakened digestive system. Medicinal herbs like ginseng, ashwagandha, and slippery elm can help alleviate these symptoms. Ginseng, for example, is known to boost energy levels and reduce fatigue. Ashwagandha, an adaptogenic herb, can help reduce stress and promote relaxation. Slippery elm, a natural demulcent, can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.  It's worth noting that while medicinal herbs can be effective in alleviating symptoms, they should be used in conjunction with medical treatment and under the guidance of a healthcare professional. Some herbs can interact with medications or worsen underlying conditions, so it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using them.
